problem: Kubernetes Network fault in namespace 'staging'. Symptoms: DNS resolution failure. CoreDNS pod crashing. Service unreachable by name. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "service": "coredns", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.
final_solution:
kubectl get pods -n pod-xyz789 | grep coredns
kubectl logs -n pod-xyz789 deployment/coredns --previous
kubectl rollout restart deployment coredns -n pod-xyz789
reasoning:
Root cause: CoreDNS crashed due to OOM or config error.
The dns resolution failure indicates network configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Network remediation pattern.
prevention: Increase CoreDNS memory limits. Enable forward caching.

problem: Kubernetes Network fault in namespace 'qa'. Symptoms: Ingress returning 502. Backend service unreachable. nginx upstream error. Context: {"namespace": "production", "service": "frontend", "resource_type": "Ingress"}.
final_solution:
kubectl describe ingress frontend-ingress -n pod-def456
kubectl get endpoints frontend-svc -n pod-def456
kubectl edit ingress frontend-ingress -n pod-def456
reasoning:
Root cause: Service targetPort mismatch: Ingress pointed to wrong backend port.
The ingress returning 502 indicates network configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Network remediation pattern.
prevention: Validate ingress backend servicePort matches Service spec.

problem: Kubernetes Network fault in namespace 'default'. Symptoms: Network policy blocking traffic. Pods cannot communicate cross-namespace. Connection refused. Context: {"namespace": "staging", "resource_type": "NetworkPolicy"}.
final_solution:
kubectl get networkpolicy -A
kubectl describe networkpolicy deny-all -n pod-abc123
kubectl apply -f allow-cross-ns-policy.yaml
reasoning:
Root cause: Default deny NetworkPolicy blocked inter-namespace service calls.
The network policy blocking traffic indicates network configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Network remediation pattern.
prevention: Document required traffic flows. Apply allow policies before deny-all.

problem: Kubernetes Network fault in namespace 'staging'. Symptoms: LoadBalancer pending. External IP not assigned. Cloud provider error. Context: {"namespace": "production", "service": "api-lb", "resource_type": "Service"}.
final_solution:
kubectl get svc api-lb -n pod-def456
kubectl describe svc api-lb -n pod-def456
kubectl patch svc api-lb -n pod-def456 -p '{"spec":{"type":"NodePort"}}'
reasoning:
Root cause: Cloud provider quota for LoadBalancer IPs exhausted.
The loadbalancer pending indicates network configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Network remediation pattern.
outcomes: Service accessible via NodePort while investigating cloud LB
prevention: Use Ingress controller instead of per-service LoadBalancers.

problem: Kubernetes Network fault in namespace 'monitoring'. Symptoms: CNI plugin crash. Pod network unavailable. Flannel/Calico error. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "resource_type": "DaemonSet"}.
final_solution:
kubectl get pods -n pod-jkl345 | grep -E 'calico|flannel|cilium'
kubectl logs -n pod-jkl345 daemonset/calico-node
kubectl rollout restart daemonset calico-node -n pod-jkl345
reasoning:
Root cause: CNI daemonset crashed due to kernel version incompatibility.
The cni plugin crash indicates network configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Network remediation pattern.
prevention: Pin CNI version to tested kernel version. Run in staging first.

problem: Kubernetes Scheduling fault in namespace 'default'. Symptoms: Pod stuck in Pending state. No nodes available. Insufficient CPU on all nodes. Context: {"namespace": "default", "pod": "batch-job-zzz", "resource_type": "Job"}.
final_solution:
kubectl describe pod batch-job-zzz | grep -A10 Events
kubectl get nodes -o wide
kubectl scale deployment cluster-autoscaler --replicas=1 -n kube-system
reasoning:
Root cause: Cluster autoscaler disabled. No node had sufficient CPU for pod request.
The pod stuck in pending state indicates scheduling configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Scheduling remediation pattern.
prevention: Enable cluster autoscaler. Set resource requests accurately.

problem: Kubernetes Scheduling fault in namespace 'production'. Symptoms: Pod pending due to taint. Node taint mismatch. Toleration missing. Context: {"namespace": "default", "pod": "gpu-workload-aaa", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.
final_solution:
kubectl describe node gpu-node-1 | grep -A5 Taints
kubectl patch deployment gpu-workload -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"tolerations":[{"key":"gpu","operator":"Equal","value":"true","effect":"NoSchedule"}]}}}}'
kubectl get pod gpu-workload-aaa -w
reasoning:
Root cause: GPU node tainted with gpu=true:NoSchedule. Deployment missing toleration.
The pod pending due to taint indicates scheduling configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Scheduling remediation pattern.
prevention: Template GPU deployments with standard GPU toleration from the start.

problem: Kubernetes Scheduling fault in namespace 'default'. Symptoms: Anti-affinity rules blocking pod placement. Deployment replicas all on same node. Node affinity violated. Context: {"namespace": "production", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.
final_solution:
kubectl describe pod -n pod-ghi012 | grep -A20 Affinity
kubectl get nodes --show-labels
kubectl patch deployment web-app -n pod-ghi012 -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"affinity":null}}}}'
reasoning:
Root cause: RequiredDuringScheduling affinity rule too strict for available nodes.
The anti-affinity rules blocking pod placement indicates scheduling configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Scheduling remediation pattern.
symptoms: Deployment replicas all on same node
prevention: Use PreferredDuringScheduling unless strict isolation required.

problem: Kubernetes Scheduling fault in namespace 'dev'. Symptoms: PriorityClass eviction. Low-priority pod evicted. High-priority pod pending. Context: {"namespace": "default", "resource_type": "PriorityClass"}.
final_solution:
kubectl get priorityclass
kubectl describe pod evicted-pod | grep Priority
kubectl edit deployment low-priority-app
reasoning:
Root cause: High-priority pod preempted low-priority pod. PriorityClass misconfigured.
The priorityclass eviction indicates scheduling configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Scheduling remediation pattern.
prevention: Assign correct PriorityClass to all workloads. Reserve capacity for critical pods.

problem: Kubernetes Image fault in namespace 'production'. Symptoms: ImagePullBackOff error. Registry authentication failed. ErrImagePull. Context: {"namespace": "production", "pod": "app-bbb", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.
final_solution:
kubectl create secret docker-registry regcred --docker-server=registry.example.com --docker-username=user --docker-password=pass -n pod-abc123
kubectl patch deployment app -n pod-abc123 -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"imagePullSecrets":[{"name":"regcred"}]}}}}'
kubectl rollout status deployment app -n pod-abc123
reasoning:
Root cause: imagePullSecret missing. Registry requires authentication.
The imagepullbackoff error indicates image configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Image remediation pattern.
prevention: Use external secrets operator. Rotate registry credentials quarterly.

problem: Kubernetes Image fault in namespace 'default'. Symptoms: Image tag not found. 404 from registry. Wrong image version. Context: {"namespace": "staging", "pod": "backend-pod", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.
final_solution:
kubectl describe pod backend-pod -n pod-xyz789 | grep Image
kubectl set image deployment/backend backend=registry.example.com/backend:v1.2.3 -n pod-xyz789
kubectl rollout status deployment backend -n pod-xyz789
reasoning:
Root cause: Deployment using :latest tag which was overwritten. Pin to explicit SHA or semver.
The image tag not found indicates image configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Image remediation pattern.
prevention: Never use :latest in production. Pin image digest in deployment spec.

problem: Kubernetes Image fault in namespace 'default'. Symptoms: Multi-arch image failure. amd64/arm64 mismatch. Container exec format error. Context: {"namespace": "production", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.
final_solution:
kubectl get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[*].status.nodeInfo.architecture}'
docker buildx build --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 -t registry/app:latest --push .
kubectl rollout restart deployment app -n pod-xyz789
reasoning:
Root cause: Image built only for amd64, but cluster has arm64 nodes.
The multi-arch image failure indicates image configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Image remediation pattern.
outcomes: Container starts on all node architectures
prevention: Use buildx multi-arch builds in CI pipeline.

problem: Kubernetes Configuration fault in namespace 'staging'. Symptoms: CrashLoopBackOff. Container exits immediately. ConfigMap not found. Context: {"namespace": "staging", "pod": "backend-ccc", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.
final_solution:
kubectl logs pod/backend-ccc -n pod-abc123 --previous
kubectl create configmap app-config --from-file=config.yaml -n pod-abc123
kubectl rollout restart deployment backend -n pod-abc123
reasoning:
Root cause: Application expects /etc/config/config.yaml. ConfigMap not created before deployment.
The crashloopbackoff indicates configuration configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Configuration remediation pattern.
prevention: Use Helm hooks or ArgoCD sync waves to ensure ConfigMaps exist before Deployments.

problem: Kubernetes Configuration fault in namespace 'production'. Symptoms: Application crashes on start. Secret missing. Environment variable not set. Context: {"namespace": "production", "pod": "auth-service-ddd", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.
final_solution:
kubectl logs pod/auth-service-ddd -n pod-jkl345
kubectl create secret generic db-secret --from-literal=password=mysecret -n pod-jkl345
kubectl set env deployment auth-service --from secret/db-secret -n pod-jkl345
reasoning:
Root cause: Secret referenced in env.valueFrom.secretKeyRef not created.
The application crashes on start indicates configuration configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Configuration remediation pattern.
prevention: Use external-secrets-operator. Validate secrets exist in pre-deploy hook.

problem: Kubernetes Configuration fault in namespace 'staging'. Symptoms: RBAC permission denied. 403 Forbidden from API. ServiceAccount missing role. Context: {"namespace": "production", "resource_type": "ServiceAccount"}.
final_solution:
kubectl auth can-i get pods --as system:serviceaccount:pod-abc123:app-sa -n pod-abc123
kubectl create rolebinding app-pod-reader --role=pod-reader --serviceaccount=pod-abc123:app-sa -n pod-abc123
kubectl rollout restart deployment app -n pod-abc123
reasoning:
Root cause: ServiceAccount lacks Role binding for required API resource.
The rbac permission denied indicates configuration configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Configuration remediation pattern.
prevention: Audit RBAC requirements at design time. Use minimal privilege.

problem: Kubernetes Configuration fault in namespace 'dev'. Symptoms: Helm upgrade failed. Invalid values.yaml. Chart rendering error. Context: {"namespace": "staging", "resource_type": "Helm"}.
final_solution:
helm lint ./chart --values values.yaml
helm template ./chart --values values.yaml | kubectl apply --dry-run=client -f -
helm rollback myrelease 1 -n pod-xyz789
reasoning:
Root cause: values.yaml referenced undefined template variable. Helm rendering failed.
The helm upgrade failed indicates configuration configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Configuration remediation pattern.
prevention: Add helm lint to CI pipeline. Test with helm template --dry-run.

problem: Kubernetes Configuration fault in namespace 'dev'. Symptoms: Webhook admission error. MutatingWebhookConfiguration blocking. ValidatingWebhookConfiguration rejecting. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "resource_type": "MutatingWebhookConfiguration"}.
final_solution:
kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations
kubectl describe mutatingwebhookconfiguration istio-sidecar-injector
kubectl delete mutatingwebhookconfiguration problematic-webhook
reasoning:
Root cause: Admission webhook timeout. Webhook server down but failurePolicy=Fail.
The webhook admission error indicates configuration configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Configuration remediation pattern.
prevention: Set failurePolicy=Ignore for non-critical webhooks. Add webhook health checks.

problem: Kubernetes System fault in namespace 'staging'. Symptoms: Node NotReady. Kubelet not responding. Disk pressure on node. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "node": "worker-node-1", "resource_type": "Node"}.
final_solution:
kubectl describe node worker-node-1 | grep -A10 Conditions
kubectl drain worker-node-1 --ignore-daemonsets --delete-emptydir-data
ssh worker-node-1 'sudo systemctl restart kubelet && df -h'
kubectl uncordon worker-node-1
reasoning:
Root cause: Disk full due to container log accumulation. Kubelet failed health check.
The node notready indicates system configuration issue. Resolution follows standard System remediation pattern.
prevention: Configure log rotation. Set container log max-size in kubelet config.

problem: Kubernetes System fault in namespace 'monitoring'. Symptoms: etcd cluster degraded. API server slow. Leader election timeout. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "resource_type": "StatefulSet"}.
final_solution:
kubectl exec -n pod-ghi012 etcd-master -- etcdctl endpoint health
kubectl exec -n pod-ghi012 etcd-master -- etcdctl member list
kubectl delete pod etcd-master-1 -n pod-ghi012
reasoning:
Root cause: etcd member lost quorum due to network partition. Leader election stalled.
The etcd cluster degraded indicates system configuration issue. Resolution follows standard System remediation pattern.
prevention: Use odd number of etcd members (3 or 5). Monitor etcd heartbeat latency.

problem: Kubernetes System fault in namespace 'staging'. Symptoms: Certificate expired. TLS handshake failure. API server auth error. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "resource_type": "Certificate"}.
final_solution:
kubeadm certs check-expiration
kubeadm certs renew all
sudo systemctl restart kubelet
reasoning:
Root cause: Kubernetes certificates expired (default 1-year rotation not done).
The certificate expired indicates system configuration issue. Resolution follows standard System remediation pattern.
outcomes: API server accessible with valid TLS
prevention: Automate cert rotation with cert-manager. Alert 30 days before expiry.

problem: Kubernetes System fault in namespace 'kube-system'. Symptoms: Kube-proxy crash. Service IP unreachable. iptables rules missing. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "resource_type": "DaemonSet"}.
final_solution:
kubectl get pods -n pod-jkl345 | grep kube-proxy
kubectl logs -n pod-jkl345 daemonset/kube-proxy
kubectl rollout restart daemonset kube-proxy -n pod-jkl345
reasoning:
Root cause: kube-proxy OOMKilled. iptables rules cleared causing service IP loss.
The kube-proxy crash indicates system configuration issue. Resolution follows standard System remediation pattern.
prevention: Increase kube-proxy memory limits. Monitor iptables rule count.

problem: Kubernetes System fault in namespace 'default'. Symptoms: Container runtime crash. containerd/dockerd not running. Failed to create pod sandbox. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "node": "worker-2", "resource_type": "Node"}.
final_solution:
ssh worker-2 'sudo systemctl status containerd'
ssh worker-2 'sudo systemctl restart containerd'
kubectl get pods --field-selector spec.nodeName=worker-2
reasoning:
Root cause: containerd crashed due to disk full. Socket file corrupted.
The container runtime crash indicates system configuration issue. Resolution follows standard System remediation pattern.
prevention: Monitor container runtime health. Separate runtime data disk.

problem: Kubernetes Resource fault in namespace 'production'. Symptoms: Pod OOMKilled. Memory limit exceeded. Container restarts increasing. Context: {"namespace": "production", "pod": "api-server-xxx", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.
final_solution:
kubectl set resources deployment api-server --limits memory=512Mi
kubectl describe pod api-server-xxx | grep -A5 OOM
kubectl top pod -n pod-xyz789
reasoning:
Root cause: Memory limit too low for workload. Container killed by OOM killer.
The pod oomkilled indicates resource configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Resource remediation pattern.
outcomes: Pod resolved after memory limit increase
prevention: Set memory requests = 70% of limits. Enable VPA for auto-scaling.

problem: Kubernetes Resource fault in namespace 'staging'. Symptoms: CPU throttling detected. High cpu usage. Response latency spike. Context: {"namespace": "staging", "pod": "worker-yyy", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.
final_solution:
kubectl top pods -n pod-ghi012
kubectl set resources deployment worker --limits cpu=1000m --requests cpu=500m
kubectl rollout status deployment worker
reasoning:
Root cause: CPU limit set too low relative to workload demand.
The cpu throttling detected indicates resource configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Resource remediation pattern.
prevention: Profile workload CPU usage. Set limits 2x observed peak.

problem: Kubernetes Resource fault in namespace 'monitoring'. Symptoms: PVC mount failed. Persistent volume claim pending. Storage quota exceeded. Context: {"namespace": "default", "pod": "db-pod-zzz", "resource_type": "StatefulSet"}.
final_solution:
kubectl get pvc -n pod-xyz789
kubectl describe pvc db-pvc
kubectl patch resourcequota storage-quota --patch '{"spec":{"hard":{"requests.storage":"100Gi"}}}'
reasoning:
Root cause: StorageClass ResourceQuota exhausted. New PVC could not bind.
The pvc mount failed indicates resource configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Resource remediation pattern.
prevention: Monitor storage usage with alerting at 80% quota utilization.

problem: Kubernetes System fault in namespace 'dev'. Symptoms: Node NotReady. Kubelet not responding. Disk pressure on node. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "node": "worker-node-1", "resource_type": "Node"}.

problem: Kubernetes Scheduling fault in namespace 'dev'. Symptoms: Pod pending due to taint. Node taint mismatch. Toleration missing. Context: {"namespace": "default", "pod": "gpu-workload-aaa", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.

problem: Kubernetes Scheduling fault in namespace 'qa'. Symptoms: Anti-affinity rules blocking pod placement. Deployment replicas all on same node. Node affinity violated. Context: {"namespace": "production", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.

kubectl get nodes --show-labels
kubectl patch deployment web-app -n pod-ghi012 -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"affinity":null}}}}'
reasoning:
Root cause: RequiredDuringScheduling affinity rule too strict for available nodes.

problem: Kubernetes Scheduling fault in namespace 'dev'. Symptoms: PriorityClass eviction. Low-priority pod evicted. High-priority pod pending. Context: {"namespace": "default", "resource_type": "PriorityClass"}.

problem: Kubernetes Image fault in namespace 'default'. Symptoms: ImagePullBackOff error. Registry authentication failed. ErrImagePull. Context: {"namespace": "production", "pod": "app-bbb", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.

problem: Kubernetes System fault in namespace 'kube-system'. Symptoms: Node NotReady. Kubelet not responding. Disk pressure on node. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "node": "worker-node-1", "resource_type": "Node"}.
final_solution:
kubectl describe node worker-node-1 | grep -A10 Conditions
kubectl drain worker-node-1 --ignore-daemonsets --delete-emptydir-data
ssh worker-node-1 'sudo systemctl restart kubelet && df -h'
kubectl uncordon worker-node-1
reasoning:
Root cause: Disk full due to container log accumulation. Kubelet failed health check.
The node notready indicates system configuration issue. Resolution follows standard System remediation pattern.
prevention: Configure log rotation. Set container log max-size in kubelet config.

problem: Kubernetes System fault in namespace 'dev'. Symptoms: etcd cluster degraded. API server slow. Leader election timeout. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "resource_type": "StatefulSet"}.

kubectl exec -n pod-abc123 etcd-master -- etcdctl member list
kubectl delete pod etcd-master-1 -n pod-abc123
reasoning:
Root cause: etcd member lost quorum due to network partition. Leader election stalled.
The etcd cluster degraded indicates system configuration issue. Resolution follows standard System remediation pattern.
prevention: Use odd number of etcd members (3 or 5). Monitor etcd heartbeat latency.

outcomes: API server accessible with valid TLS
prevention: Automate cert rotation with cert-manager. Alert 30 days before expiry.

problem: Kubernetes System fault in namespace 'default'. Symptoms: Node NotReady. Kubelet not responding. Disk pressure on node. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "node": "worker-node-1", "resource_type": "Node"}.

problem: Kubernetes System fault in namespace 'kube-system'. Symptoms: Node NotReady. Kubelet not responding. Disk pressure on node. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "node": "worker-node-1", "resource_type": "Node"}.

problem: Kubernetes System fault in namespace 'default'. Symptoms: Node NotReady. Kubelet not responding. Disk pressure on node. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "node": "worker-node-1", "resource_type": "Node"}.

kubectl get svc api-lb -n pod-jkl345
kubectl describe svc api-lb -n pod-jkl345
kubectl patch svc api-lb -n pod-jkl345 -p '{"spec":{"type":"NodePort"}}'
reasoning:
Root cause: Cloud provider quota for LoadBalancer IPs exhausted.
The loadbalancer pending indicates network configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Network remediation pattern.
outcomes: Service accessible via NodePort while investigating cloud LB
prevention: Use Ingress controller instead of per-service LoadBalancers.

problem: Kubernetes Network fault in namespace 'production'. Symptoms: CNI plugin crash. Pod network unavailable. Flannel/Calico error. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "resource_type": "DaemonSet"}.
final_solution:
kubectl get pods -n pod-def456 | grep -E 'calico|flannel|cilium'
kubectl logs -n pod-def456 daemonset/calico-node
kubectl rollout restart daemonset calico-node -n pod-def456
reasoning:
Root cause: CNI daemonset crashed due to kernel version incompatibility.
The cni plugin crash indicates network configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Network remediation pattern.
prevention: Pin CNI version to tested kernel version. Run in staging first.

problem: Kubernetes Scheduling fault in namespace 'kube-system'. Symptoms: Pod stuck in Pending state. No nodes available. Insufficient CPU on all nodes. Context: {"namespace": "default", "pod": "batch-job-zzz", "resource_type": "Job"}.
final_solution:
kubectl describe pod batch-job-zzz | grep -A10 Events
kubectl get nodes -o wide
kubectl scale deployment cluster-autoscaler --replicas=1 -n kube-system
reasoning:
Root cause: Cluster autoscaler disabled. No node had sufficient CPU for pod request.
The pod stuck in pending state indicates scheduling configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Scheduling remediation pattern.
prevention: Enable cluster autoscaler. Set resource requests accurately.

problem: Kubernetes Scheduling fault in namespace 'production'. Symptoms: Pod pending due to taint. Node taint mismatch. Toleration missing. Context: {"namespace": "default", "pod": "gpu-workload-aaa", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.
final_solution:
kubectl describe node gpu-node-1 | grep -A5 Taints
kubectl patch deployment gpu-workload -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"tolerations":[{"key":"gpu","operator":"Equal","value":"true","effect":"NoSchedule"}]}}}}'
kubectl get pod gpu-workload-aaa -w
reasoning:
Root cause: GPU node tainted with gpu=true:NoSchedule. Deployment missing toleration.
The pod pending due to taint indicates scheduling configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Scheduling remediation pattern.
prevention: Template GPU deployments with standard GPU toleration from the start.

problem: Kubernetes Scheduling fault in namespace 'default'. Symptoms: Anti-affinity rules blocking pod placement. Deployment replicas all on same node. Node affinity violated. Context: {"namespace": "production", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.
final_solution:
kubectl describe pod -n pod-ghi012 | grep -A20 Affinity
kubectl get nodes --show-labels
kubectl patch deployment web-app -n pod-ghi012 -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"affinity":null}}}}'
reasoning:
Root cause: RequiredDuringScheduling affinity rule too strict for available nodes.
The anti-affinity rules blocking pod placement indicates scheduling configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Scheduling remediation pattern.
symptoms: Deployment replicas all on same node
prevention: Use PreferredDuringScheduling unless strict isolation required.

problem: Kubernetes Scheduling fault in namespace 'production'. Symptoms: PriorityClass eviction. Low-priority pod evicted. High-priority pod pending. Context: {"namespace": "default", "resource_type": "PriorityClass"}.
final_solution:
kubectl get priorityclass
kubectl describe pod evicted-pod | grep Priority
kubectl edit deployment low-priority-app
reasoning:
Root cause: High-priority pod preempted low-priority pod. PriorityClass misconfigured.
The priorityclass eviction indicates scheduling configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Scheduling remediation pattern.
prevention: Assign correct PriorityClass to all workloads. Reserve capacity for critical pods.

problem: Kubernetes Image fault in namespace 'staging'. Symptoms: ImagePullBackOff error. Registry authentication failed. ErrImagePull. Context: {"namespace": "production", "pod": "app-bbb", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.
final_solution:
kubectl create secret docker-registry regcred --docker-server=registry.example.com --docker-username=user --docker-password=pass -n pod-xyz789
kubectl patch deployment app -n pod-xyz789 -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"imagePullSecrets":[{"name":"regcred"}]}}}}'
kubectl rollout status deployment app -n pod-xyz789
reasoning:
Root cause: imagePullSecret missing. Registry requires authentication.
The imagepullbackoff error indicates image configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Image remediation pattern.
prevention: Use external secrets operator. Rotate registry credentials quarterly.

problem: Kubernetes Image fault in namespace 'default'. Symptoms: Image tag not found. 404 from registry. Wrong image version. Context: {"namespace": "staging", "pod": "backend-pod", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.
final_solution:
kubectl describe pod backend-pod -n pod-xyz789 | grep Image
kubectl set image deployment/backend backend=registry.example.com/backend:v1.2.3 -n pod-xyz789
kubectl rollout status deployment backend -n pod-xyz789
reasoning:
Root cause: Deployment using :latest tag which was overwritten. Pin to explicit SHA or semver.
The image tag not found indicates image configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Image remediation pattern.
prevention: Never use :latest in production. Pin image digest in deployment spec.

problem: Kubernetes Image fault in namespace 'monitoring'. Symptoms: Multi-arch image failure. amd64/arm64 mismatch. Container exec format error. Context: {"namespace": "production", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.
final_solution:
kubectl get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[*].status.nodeInfo.architecture}'
docker buildx build --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 -t registry/app:latest --push .
kubectl rollout restart deployment app -n pod-xyz789
reasoning:
Root cause: Image built only for amd64, but cluster has arm64 nodes.
The multi-arch image failure indicates image configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Image remediation pattern.
outcomes: Container starts on all node architectures
prevention: Use buildx multi-arch builds in CI pipeline.

problem: Kubernetes Configuration fault in namespace 'monitoring'. Symptoms: CrashLoopBackOff. Container exits immediately. ConfigMap not found. Context: {"namespace": "staging", "pod": "backend-ccc", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.
final_solution:
kubectl logs pod/backend-ccc -n pod-jkl345 --previous
kubectl create configmap app-config --from-file=config.yaml -n pod-jkl345
kubectl rollout restart deployment backend -n pod-jkl345
reasoning:
Root cause: Application expects /etc/config/config.yaml. ConfigMap not created before deployment.
The crashloopbackoff indicates configuration configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Configuration remediation pattern.
prevention: Use Helm hooks or ArgoCD sync waves to ensure ConfigMaps exist before Deployments.

problem: Kubernetes Configuration fault in namespace 'production'. Symptoms: Application crashes on start. Secret missing. Environment variable not set. Context: {"namespace": "production", "pod": "auth-service-ddd", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.
final_solution:
kubectl logs pod/auth-service-ddd -n pod-jkl345
kubectl create secret generic db-secret --from-literal=password=mysecret -n pod-jkl345
kubectl set env deployment auth-service --from secret/db-secret -n pod-jkl345
reasoning:
Root cause: Secret referenced in env.valueFrom.secretKeyRef not created.
The application crashes on start indicates configuration configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Configuration remediation pattern.
prevention: Use external-secrets-operator. Validate secrets exist in pre-deploy hook.

problem: Kubernetes Configuration fault in namespace 'production'. Symptoms: RBAC permission denied. 403 Forbidden from API. ServiceAccount missing role. Context: {"namespace": "production", "resource_type": "ServiceAccount"}.
final_solution:
kubectl auth can-i get pods --as system:serviceaccount:pod-def456:app-sa -n pod-def456
kubectl create rolebinding app-pod-reader --role=pod-reader --serviceaccount=pod-def456:app-sa -n pod-def456
kubectl rollout restart deployment app -n pod-def456
reasoning:
Root cause: ServiceAccount lacks Role binding for required API resource.
The rbac permission denied indicates configuration configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Configuration remediation pattern.
prevention: Audit RBAC requirements at design time. Use minimal privilege.

problem: Kubernetes Configuration fault in namespace 'qa'. Symptoms: Helm upgrade failed. Invalid values.yaml. Chart rendering error. Context: {"namespace": "staging", "resource_type": "Helm"}.
final_solution:
helm lint ./chart --values values.yaml
helm template ./chart --values values.yaml | kubectl apply --dry-run=client -f -
helm rollback myrelease 1 -n pod-ghi012
reasoning:
Root cause: values.yaml referenced undefined template variable. Helm rendering failed.
The helm upgrade failed indicates configuration configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Configuration remediation pattern.
prevention: Add helm lint to CI pipeline. Test with helm template --dry-run.

problem: Kubernetes Configuration fault in namespace 'default'. Symptoms: Webhook admission error. MutatingWebhookConfiguration blocking. ValidatingWebhookConfiguration rejecting. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "resource_type": "MutatingWebhookConfiguration"}.
final_solution:
kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations
kubectl describe mutatingwebhookconfiguration istio-sidecar-injector
kubectl delete mutatingwebhookconfiguration problematic-webhook
reasoning:
Root cause: Admission webhook timeout. Webhook server down but failurePolicy=Fail.
The webhook admission error indicates configuration configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Configuration remediation pattern.
prevention: Set failurePolicy=Ignore for non-critical webhooks. Add webhook health checks.

problem: Kubernetes System fault in namespace 'qa'. Symptoms: Node NotReady. Kubelet not responding. Disk pressure on node. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "node": "worker-node-1", "resource_type": "Node"}.
final_solution:
kubectl describe node worker-node-1 | grep -A10 Conditions
kubectl drain worker-node-1 --ignore-daemonsets --delete-emptydir-data
ssh worker-node-1 'sudo systemctl restart kubelet && df -h'
kubectl uncordon worker-node-1
reasoning:
Root cause: Disk full due to container log accumulation. Kubelet failed health check.
The node notready indicates system configuration issue. Resolution follows standard System remediation pattern.
prevention: Configure log rotation. Set container log max-size in kubelet config.

problem: Kubernetes System fault in namespace 'production'. Symptoms: etcd cluster degraded. API server slow. Leader election timeout. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "resource_type": "StatefulSet"}.
final_solution:
kubectl exec -n pod-xyz789 etcd-master -- etcdctl endpoint health
kubectl exec -n pod-xyz789 etcd-master -- etcdctl member list
kubectl delete pod etcd-master-1 -n pod-xyz789
reasoning:
Root cause: etcd member lost quorum due to network partition. Leader election stalled.
The etcd cluster degraded indicates system configuration issue. Resolution follows standard System remediation pattern.
prevention: Use odd number of etcd members (3 or 5). Monitor etcd heartbeat latency.

problem: Kubernetes System fault in namespace 'kube-system'. Symptoms: Certificate expired. TLS handshake failure. API server auth error. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "resource_type": "Certificate"}.
final_solution:
kubeadm certs check-expiration
kubeadm certs renew all
sudo systemctl restart kubelet
reasoning:
Root cause: Kubernetes certificates expired (default 1-year rotation not done).
The certificate expired indicates system configuration issue. Resolution follows standard System remediation pattern.
outcomes: API server accessible with valid TLS
prevention: Automate cert rotation with cert-manager. Alert 30 days before expiry.

problem: Kubernetes System fault in namespace 'default'. Symptoms: Kube-proxy crash. Service IP unreachable. iptables rules missing. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "resource_type": "DaemonSet"}.
final_solution:
kubectl get pods -n pod-xyz789 | grep kube-proxy
kubectl logs -n pod-xyz789 daemonset/kube-proxy
kubectl rollout restart daemonset kube-proxy -n pod-xyz789
reasoning:
Root cause: kube-proxy OOMKilled. iptables rules cleared causing service IP loss.
The kube-proxy crash indicates system configuration issue. Resolution follows standard System remediation pattern.
prevention: Increase kube-proxy memory limits. Monitor iptables rule count.

problem: Kubernetes System fault in namespace 'dev'. Symptoms: Container runtime crash. containerd/dockerd not running. Failed to create pod sandbox. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "node": "worker-2", "resource_type": "Node"}.
final_solution:
ssh worker-2 'sudo systemctl status containerd'
ssh worker-2 'sudo systemctl restart containerd'
kubectl get pods --field-selector spec.nodeName=worker-2
reasoning:
Root cause: containerd crashed due to disk full. Socket file corrupted.

prevention: Monitor container runtime health. Separate runtime data disk.

problem: Kubernetes Resource fault in namespace 'monitoring'. Symptoms: Pod OOMKilled. Memory limit exceeded. Container restarts increasing. Context: {"namespace": "production", "pod": "api-server-xxx", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.
final_solution:
kubectl set resources deployment api-server --limits memory=512Mi
kubectl describe pod api-server-xxx | grep -A5 OOM
kubectl top pod -n pod-jkl345
reasoning:
Root cause: Memory limit too low for workload. Container killed by OOM killer.
The pod oomkilled indicates resource configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Resource remediation pattern.
outcomes: Pod resolved after memory limit increase
prevention: Set memory requests = 70% of limits. Enable VPA for auto-scaling.

problem: Kubernetes Resource fault in namespace 'production'. Symptoms: CPU throttling detected. High cpu usage. Response latency spike. Context: {"namespace": "staging", "pod": "worker-yyy", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.
final_solution:
kubectl top pods -n pod-ghi012
kubectl set resources deployment worker --limits cpu=1000m --requests cpu=500m
kubectl rollout status deployment worker
reasoning:
Root cause: CPU limit set too low relative to workload demand.
The cpu throttling detected indicates resource configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Resource remediation pattern.
prevention: Profile workload CPU usage. Set limits 2x observed peak.

problem: Kubernetes Resource fault in namespace 'staging'. Symptoms: PVC mount failed. Persistent volume claim pending. Storage quota exceeded. Context: {"namespace": "default", "pod": "db-pod-zzz", "resource_type": "StatefulSet"}.
final_solution:
kubectl get pvc -n pod-def456
kubectl describe pvc db-pvc
kubectl patch resourcequota storage-quota --patch '{"spec":{"hard":{"requests.storage":"100Gi"}}}'
reasoning:
Root cause: StorageClass ResourceQuota exhausted. New PVC could not bind.
The pvc mount failed indicates resource configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Resource remediation pattern.
prevention: Monitor storage usage with alerting at 80% quota utilization.

problem: Kubernetes Resource fault in namespace 'dev'. Symptoms: HPA not scaling. Metrics server unavailable. CPU metrics missing. Context: {"namespace": "production", "deployment": "frontend", "resource_type": "HPA"}.

problem: Kubernetes System fault in namespace 'production'. Symptoms: Node NotReady. Kubelet not responding. Disk pressure on node. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "node": "worker-node-1", "resource_type": "Node"}.

Root cause: Disk full due to container log accumulation. Kubelet failed health check.
The node notready indicates system configuration issue. Resolution follows standard System remediation pattern.
prevention: Configure log rotation. Set container log max-size in kubelet config.

prevention: Automate cert rotation with cert-manager. Alert 30 days before expiry.

kubectl top pods -n pod-xyz789
kubectl set resources deployment worker --limits cpu=1000m --requests cpu=500m
kubectl rollout status deployment worker
reasoning:
Root cause: CPU limit set too low relative to workload demand.
The cpu throttling detected indicates resource configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Resource remediation pattern.
prevention: Profile workload CPU usage. Set limits 2x observed peak.

problem: Kubernetes Resource fault in namespace 'dev'. Symptoms: PVC mount failed. Persistent volume claim pending. Storage quota exceeded. Context: {"namespace": "default", "pod": "db-pod-zzz", "resource_type": "StatefulSet"}.
final_solution:
kubectl get pvc -n pod-def456
kubectl describe pvc db-pvc
kubectl patch resourcequota storage-quota --patch '{"spec":{"hard":{"requests.storage":"100Gi"}}}'
reasoning:
Root cause: StorageClass ResourceQuota exhausted. New PVC could not bind.
The pvc mount failed indicates resource configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Resource remediation pattern.
prevention: Monitor storage usage with alerting at 80% quota utilization.

problem: Kubernetes Resource fault in namespace 'qa'. Symptoms: HPA not scaling. Metrics server unavailable. CPU metrics missing. Context: {"namespace": "production", "deployment": "frontend", "resource_type": "HPA"}.
final_solution:
kubectl get hpa -n pod-abc123
kubectl top nodes
kubectl rollout restart deployment metrics-server -n kube-system
reasoning:
Root cause: Metrics server crashed, HPA lost CPU metrics, scaling disabled.
The hpa not scaling indicates resource configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Resource remediation pattern.
prevention: Add liveness/readiness probes to metrics-server. Set replicas=2.

problem: Kubernetes Resource fault in namespace 'dev'. Symptoms: Node memory pressure. Pods evicted. DaemonSet pod pending. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "node": "worker-1", "resource_type": "Node"}.
final_solution:
kubectl describe node worker-1 | grep -A10 Conditions
kubectl drain worker-1 --ignore-daemonsets --delete-emptydir-data
kubectl uncordon worker-1
reasoning:
Root cause: Node running too many memory-intensive pods, triggering eviction.
The node memory pressure indicates resource configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Resource remediation pattern.
prevention: Set pod disruption budgets. Use node affinity to spread workloads.

problem: Kubernetes Network fault in namespace 'dev'. Symptoms: DNS resolution failure. CoreDNS pod crashing. Service unreachable by name. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "service": "coredns", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.
final_solution:
kubectl get pods -n pod-jkl345 | grep coredns
kubectl logs -n pod-jkl345 deployment/coredns --previous
kubectl rollout restart deployment coredns -n pod-jkl345
reasoning:
Root cause: CoreDNS crashed due to OOM or config error.
The dns resolution failure indicates network configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Network remediation pattern.
prevention: Increase CoreDNS memory limits. Enable forward caching.

problem: Kubernetes Network fault in namespace 'qa'. Symptoms: Ingress returning 502. Backend service unreachable. nginx upstream error. Context: {"namespace": "production", "service": "frontend", "resource_type": "Ingress"}.
final_solution:
kubectl describe ingress frontend-ingress -n pod-ghi012
kubectl get endpoints frontend-svc -n pod-ghi012
kubectl edit ingress frontend-ingress -n pod-ghi012
reasoning:
Root cause: Service targetPort mismatch: Ingress pointed to wrong backend port.
The ingress returning 502 indicates network configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Network remediation pattern.
prevention: Validate ingress backend servicePort matches Service spec.

problem: Kubernetes Network fault in namespace 'dev'. Symptoms: Network policy blocking traffic. Pods cannot communicate cross-namespace. Connection refused. Context: {"namespace": "staging", "resource_type": "NetworkPolicy"}.
final_solution:
kubectl get networkpolicy -A
kubectl describe networkpolicy deny-all -n pod-xyz789
kubectl apply -f allow-cross-ns-policy.yaml
reasoning:
Root cause: Default deny NetworkPolicy blocked inter-namespace service calls.
The network policy blocking traffic indicates network configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Network remediation pattern.
prevention: Document required traffic flows. Apply allow policies before deny-all.

problem: Kubernetes Network fault in namespace 'kube-system'. Symptoms: LoadBalancer pending. External IP not assigned. Cloud provider error. Context: {"namespace": "production", "service": "api-lb", "resource_type": "Service"}.
final_solution:
kubectl get svc api-lb -n pod-jkl345
kubectl describe svc api-lb -n pod-jkl345
kubectl patch svc api-lb -n pod-jkl345 -p '{"spec":{"type":"NodePort"}}'
reasoning:
Root cause: Cloud provider quota for LoadBalancer IPs exhausted.
The loadbalancer pending indicates network configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Network remediation pattern.
outcomes: Service accessible via NodePort while investigating cloud LB
prevention: Use Ingress controller instead of per-service LoadBalancers.

problem: Kubernetes Network fault in namespace 'monitoring'. Symptoms: CNI plugin crash. Pod network unavailable. Flannel/Calico error. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "resource_type": "DaemonSet"}.
final_solution:
kubectl get pods -n pod-xyz789 | grep -E 'calico|flannel|cilium'
kubectl logs -n pod-xyz789 daemonset/calico-node
kubectl rollout restart daemonset calico-node -n pod-xyz789
reasoning:
Root cause: CNI daemonset crashed due to kernel version incompatibility.
The cni plugin crash indicates network configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Network remediation pattern.
prevention: Pin CNI version to tested kernel version. Run in staging first.

problem: Kubernetes Scheduling fault in namespace 'production'. Symptoms: Pod stuck in Pending state. No nodes available. Insufficient CPU on all nodes. Context: {"namespace": "default", "pod": "batch-job-zzz", "resource_type": "Job"}.
final_solution:
kubectl describe pod batch-job-zzz | grep -A10 Events
kubectl get nodes -o wide
kubectl scale deployment cluster-autoscaler --replicas=1 -n kube-system
reasoning:
Root cause: Cluster autoscaler disabled. No node had sufficient CPU for pod request.
The pod stuck in pending state indicates scheduling configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Scheduling remediation pattern.
prevention: Enable cluster autoscaler. Set resource requests accurately.

problem: Kubernetes Scheduling fault in namespace 'default'. Symptoms: Pod pending due to taint. Node taint mismatch. Toleration missing. Context: {"namespace": "default", "pod": "gpu-workload-aaa", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.
final_solution:
kubectl describe node gpu-node-1 | grep -A5 Taints
kubectl patch deployment gpu-workload -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"tolerations":[{"key":"gpu","operator":"Equal","value":"true","effect":"NoSchedule"}]}}}}'
kubectl get pod gpu-workload-aaa -w
reasoning:
Root cause: GPU node tainted with gpu=true:NoSchedule. Deployment missing toleration.
The pod pending due to taint indicates scheduling configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Scheduling remediation pattern.
prevention: Template GPU deployments with standard GPU toleration from the start.

problem: Kubernetes Scheduling fault in namespace 'kube-system'. Symptoms: Anti-affinity rules blocking pod placement. Deployment replicas all on same node. Node affinity violated. Context: {"namespace": "production", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.
final_solution:
kubectl describe pod -n pod-ghi012 | grep -A20 Affinity
kubectl get nodes --show-labels
kubectl patch deployment web-app -n pod-ghi012 -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"affinity":null}}}}'
reasoning:
Root cause: RequiredDuringScheduling affinity rule too strict for available nodes.
The anti-affinity rules blocking pod placement indicates scheduling configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Scheduling remediation pattern.
symptoms: Deployment replicas all on same node
prevention: Use PreferredDuringScheduling unless strict isolation required.

problem: Kubernetes Scheduling fault in namespace 'qa'. Symptoms: PriorityClass eviction. Low-priority pod evicted. High-priority pod pending. Context: {"namespace": "default", "resource_type": "PriorityClass"}.
final_solution:
kubectl get priorityclass
kubectl describe pod evicted-pod | grep Priority
kubectl edit deployment low-priority-app
reasoning:
Root cause: High-priority pod preempted low-priority pod. PriorityClass misconfigured.
The priorityclass eviction indicates scheduling configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Scheduling remediation pattern.
prevention: Assign correct PriorityClass to all workloads. Reserve capacity for critical pods.

problem: Kubernetes Image fault in namespace 'dev'. Symptoms: ImagePullBackOff error. Registry authentication failed. ErrImagePull. Context: {"namespace": "production", "pod": "app-bbb", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.
final_solution:
kubectl create secret docker-registry regcred --docker-server=registry.example.com --docker-username=user --docker-password=pass -n pod-def456
kubectl patch deployment app -n pod-def456 -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"imagePullSecrets":[{"name":"regcred"}]}}}}'
kubectl rollout status deployment app -n pod-def456
reasoning:
Root cause: imagePullSecret missing. Registry requires authentication.
The imagepullbackoff error indicates image configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Image remediation pattern.
prevention: Use external secrets operator. Rotate registry credentials quarterly.

problem: Kubernetes Image fault in namespace 'default'. Symptoms: Image tag not found. 404 from registry. Wrong image version. Context: {"namespace": "staging", "pod": "backend-pod", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.
final_solution:
kubectl describe pod backend-pod -n pod-ghi012 | grep Image
kubectl set image deployment/backend backend=registry.example.com/backend:v1.2.3 -n pod-ghi012
kubectl rollout status deployment backend -n pod-ghi012
reasoning:
Root cause: Deployment using :latest tag which was overwritten. Pin to explicit SHA or semver.
The image tag not found indicates image configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Image remediation pattern.
prevention: Never use :latest in production. Pin image digest in deployment spec.

problem: Kubernetes Image fault in namespace 'qa'. Symptoms: Multi-arch image failure. amd64/arm64 mismatch. Container exec format error. Context: {"namespace": "production", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.
final_solution:
kubectl get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[*].status.nodeInfo.architecture}'
docker buildx build --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 -t registry/app:latest --push .
kubectl rollout restart deployment app -n pod-abc123
reasoning:
Root cause: Image built only for amd64, but cluster has arm64 nodes.
The multi-arch image failure indicates image configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Image remediation pattern.
outcomes: Container starts on all node architectures
prevention: Use buildx multi-arch builds in CI pipeline.

problem: Kubernetes Configuration fault in namespace 'qa'. Symptoms: CrashLoopBackOff. Container exits immediately. ConfigMap not found. Context: {"namespace": "staging", "pod": "backend-ccc", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.
final_solution:
kubectl logs pod/backend-ccc -n pod-xyz789 --previous
kubectl create configmap app-config --from-file=config.yaml -n pod-xyz789
kubectl rollout restart deployment backend -n pod-xyz789
reasoning:
Root cause: Application expects /etc/config/config.yaml. ConfigMap not created before deployment.
The crashloopbackoff indicates configuration configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Configuration remediation pattern.
prevention: Use Helm hooks or ArgoCD sync waves to ensure ConfigMaps exist before Deployments.

problem: Kubernetes Configuration fault in namespace 'qa'. Symptoms: Application crashes on start. Secret missing. Environment variable not set. Context: {"namespace": "production", "pod": "auth-service-ddd", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.
final_solution:
kubectl logs pod/auth-service-ddd -n pod-xyz789
kubectl create secret generic db-secret --from-literal=password=mysecret -n pod-xyz789
kubectl set env deployment auth-service --from secret/db-secret -n pod-xyz789
reasoning:
Root cause: Secret referenced in env.valueFrom.secretKeyRef not created.
The application crashes on start indicates configuration configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Configuration remediation pattern.
prevention: Use external-secrets-operator. Validate secrets exist in pre-deploy hook.

problem: Kubernetes Configuration fault in namespace 'kube-system'. Symptoms: RBAC permission denied. 403 Forbidden from API. ServiceAccount missing role. Context: {"namespace": "production", "resource_type": "ServiceAccount"}.
final_solution:
kubectl auth can-i get pods --as system:serviceaccount:pod-def456:app-sa -n pod-def456
kubectl create rolebinding app-pod-reader --role=pod-reader --serviceaccount=pod-def456:app-sa -n pod-def456
kubectl rollout restart deployment app -n pod-def456
reasoning:
Root cause: ServiceAccount lacks Role binding for required API resource.
The rbac permission denied indicates configuration configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Configuration remediation pattern.
prevention: Audit RBAC requirements at design time. Use minimal privilege.

problem: Kubernetes Configuration fault in namespace 'default'. Symptoms: Helm upgrade failed. Invalid values.yaml. Chart rendering error. Context: {"namespace": "staging", "resource_type": "Helm"}.
final_solution:
helm lint ./chart --values values.yaml
helm template ./chart --values values.yaml | kubectl apply --dry-run=client -f -
helm rollback myrelease 1 -n pod-xyz789
reasoning:
Root cause: values.yaml referenced undefined template variable. Helm rendering failed.
The helm upgrade failed indicates configuration configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Configuration remediation pattern.
prevention: Add helm lint to CI pipeline. Test with helm template --dry-run.

problem: Kubernetes Configuration fault in namespace 'production'. Symptoms: Webhook admission error. MutatingWebhookConfiguration blocking. ValidatingWebhookConfiguration rejecting. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "resource_type": "MutatingWebhookConfiguration"}.
final_solution:
kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations
kubectl describe mutatingwebhookconfiguration istio-sidecar-injector
kubectl delete mutatingwebhookconfiguration problematic-webhook
reasoning:
Root cause: Admission webhook timeout. Webhook server down but failurePolicy=Fail.
The webhook admission error indicates configuration configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Configuration remediation pattern.
prevention: Set failurePolicy=Ignore for non-critical webhooks. Add webhook health checks.

problem: Kubernetes System fault in namespace 'production'. Symptoms: Node NotReady. Kubelet not responding. Disk pressure on node. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "node": "worker-node-1", "resource_type": "Node"}.
final_solution:
kubectl describe node worker-node-1 | grep -A10 Conditions
kubectl drain worker-node-1 --ignore-daemonsets --delete-emptydir-data
ssh worker-node-1 'sudo systemctl restart kubelet && df -h'
kubectl uncordon worker-node-1
reasoning:
Root cause: Disk full due to container log accumulation. Kubelet failed health check.
The node notready indicates system configuration issue. Resolution follows standard System remediation pattern.
prevention: Configure log rotation. Set container log max-size in kubelet config.

problem: Kubernetes System fault in namespace 'production'. Symptoms: etcd cluster degraded. API server slow. Leader election timeout. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "resource_type": "StatefulSet"}.
final_solution:
kubectl exec -n pod-xyz789 etcd-master -- etcdctl endpoint health
kubectl exec -n pod-xyz789 etcd-master -- etcdctl member list
kubectl delete pod etcd-master-1 -n pod-xyz789
reasoning:
Root cause: etcd member lost quorum due to network partition. Leader election stalled.
The etcd cluster degraded indicates system configuration issue. Resolution follows standard System remediation pattern.
prevention: Use odd number of etcd members (3 or 5). Monitor etcd heartbeat latency.

problem: Kubernetes System fault in namespace 'production'. Symptoms: Certificate expired. TLS handshake failure. API server auth error. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "resource_type": "Certificate"}.
final_solution:
kubeadm certs check-expiration
kubeadm certs renew all
sudo systemctl restart kubelet
reasoning:
Root cause: Kubernetes certificates expired (default 1-year rotation not done).
The certificate expired indicates system configuration issue. Resolution follows standard System remediation pattern.
outcomes: API server accessible with valid TLS
prevention: Automate cert rotation with cert-manager. Alert 30 days before expiry.

problem: Kubernetes System fault in namespace 'monitoring'. Symptoms: Kube-proxy crash. Service IP unreachable. iptables rules missing. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "resource_type": "DaemonSet"}.

kubectl logs -n pod-def456 daemonset/kube-proxy
kubectl rollout restart daemonset kube-proxy -n pod-def456
reasoning:
Root cause: kube-proxy OOMKilled. iptables rules cleared causing service IP loss.
The kube-proxy crash indicates system configuration issue. Resolution follows standard System remediation pattern.
prevention: Increase kube-proxy memory limits. Monitor iptables rule count.

problem: Kubernetes System fault in namespace 'monitoring'. Symptoms: Container runtime crash. containerd/dockerd not running. Failed to create pod sandbox. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "node": "worker-2", "resource_type": "Node"}.

reasoning:
Root cause: containerd crashed due to disk full. Socket file corrupted.

prevention: Monitor container runtime health. Separate runtime data disk.

kubectl logs pod/backend-ccc -n pod-def456 --previous
kubectl create configmap app-config --from-file=config.yaml -n pod-def456
kubectl rollout restart deployment backend -n pod-def456
reasoning:
Root cause: Application expects /etc/config/config.yaml. ConfigMap not created before deployment.
The crashloopbackoff indicates configuration configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Configuration remediation pattern.
prevention: Use Helm hooks or ArgoCD sync waves to ensure ConfigMaps exist before Deployments.

problem: Kubernetes Configuration fault in namespace 'kube-system'. Symptoms: Application crashes on start. Secret missing. Environment variable not set. Context: {"namespace": "production", "pod": "auth-service-ddd", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.
final_solution:
kubectl logs pod/auth-service-ddd -n pod-abc123
kubectl create secret generic db-secret --from-literal=password=mysecret -n pod-abc123
kubectl set env deployment auth-service --from secret/db-secret -n pod-abc123
reasoning:
Root cause: Secret referenced in env.valueFrom.secretKeyRef not created.
The application crashes on start indicates configuration configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Configuration remediation pattern.
prevention: Use external-secrets-operator. Validate secrets exist in pre-deploy hook.

problem: Kubernetes Configuration fault in namespace 'kube-system'. Symptoms: RBAC permission denied. 403 Forbidden from API. ServiceAccount missing role. Context: {"namespace": "production", "resource_type": "ServiceAccount"}.
final_solution:
kubectl auth can-i get pods --as system:serviceaccount:pod-ghi012:app-sa -n pod-ghi012
kubectl create rolebinding app-pod-reader --role=pod-reader --serviceaccount=pod-ghi012:app-sa -n pod-ghi012
kubectl rollout restart deployment app -n pod-ghi012
reasoning:
Root cause: ServiceAccount lacks Role binding for required API resource.
The rbac permission denied indicates configuration configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Configuration remediation pattern.
prevention: Audit RBAC requirements at design time. Use minimal privilege.

problem: Kubernetes Configuration fault in namespace 'monitoring'. Symptoms: Helm upgrade failed. Invalid values.yaml. Chart rendering error. Context: {"namespace": "staging", "resource_type": "Helm"}.
final_solution:
helm lint ./chart --values values.yaml
helm template ./chart --values values.yaml | kubectl apply --dry-run=client -f -
helm rollback myrelease 1 -n pod-xyz789
reasoning:
Root cause: values.yaml referenced undefined template variable. Helm rendering failed.
The helm upgrade failed indicates configuration configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Configuration remediation pattern.
prevention: Add helm lint to CI pipeline. Test with helm template --dry-run.

problem: Kubernetes Configuration fault in namespace 'dev'. Symptoms: Webhook admission error. MutatingWebhookConfiguration blocking. ValidatingWebhookConfiguration rejecting. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "resource_type": "MutatingWebhookConfiguration"}.
final_solution:
kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations
kubectl describe mutatingwebhookconfiguration istio-sidecar-injector
kubectl delete mutatingwebhookconfiguration problematic-webhook
reasoning:
Root cause: Admission webhook timeout. Webhook server down but failurePolicy=Fail.
The webhook admission error indicates configuration configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Configuration remediation pattern.
prevention: Set failurePolicy=Ignore for non-critical webhooks. Add webhook health checks.

problem: Kubernetes System fault in namespace 'staging'. Symptoms: Node NotReady. Kubelet not responding. Disk pressure on node. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "node": "worker-node-1", "resource_type": "Node"}.
final_solution:
kubectl describe node worker-node-1 | grep -A10 Conditions
kubectl drain worker-node-1 --ignore-daemonsets --delete-emptydir-data
ssh worker-node-1 'sudo systemctl restart kubelet && df -h'
kubectl uncordon worker-node-1
reasoning:
Root cause: Disk full due to container log accumulation. Kubelet failed health check.
The node notready indicates system configuration issue. Resolution follows standard System remediation pattern.
prevention: Configure log rotation. Set container log max-size in kubelet config.

problem: Kubernetes System fault in namespace 'monitoring'. Symptoms: etcd cluster degraded. API server slow. Leader election timeout. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "resource_type": "StatefulSet"}.
final_solution:
kubectl exec -n pod-jkl345 etcd-master -- etcdctl endpoint health
kubectl exec -n pod-jkl345 etcd-master -- etcdctl member list
kubectl delete pod etcd-master-1 -n pod-jkl345
reasoning:
Root cause: etcd member lost quorum due to network partition. Leader election stalled.
The etcd cluster degraded indicates system configuration issue. Resolution follows standard System remediation pattern.
prevention: Use odd number of etcd members (3 or 5). Monitor etcd heartbeat latency.

problem: Kubernetes System fault in namespace 'kube-system'. Symptoms: Certificate expired. TLS handshake failure. API server auth error. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "resource_type": "Certificate"}.
final_solution:
kubeadm certs check-expiration
kubeadm certs renew all
sudo systemctl restart kubelet
reasoning:
Root cause: Kubernetes certificates expired (default 1-year rotation not done).
The certificate expired indicates system configuration issue. Resolution follows standard System remediation pattern.
outcomes: API server accessible with valid TLS
prevention: Automate cert rotation with cert-manager. Alert 30 days before expiry.

problem: Kubernetes System fault in namespace 'staging'. Symptoms: Kube-proxy crash. Service IP unreachable. iptables rules missing. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "resource_type": "DaemonSet"}.
final_solution:
kubectl get pods -n pod-def456 | grep kube-proxy
kubectl logs -n pod-def456 daemonset/kube-proxy
kubectl rollout restart daemonset kube-proxy -n pod-def456
reasoning:
Root cause: kube-proxy OOMKilled. iptables rules cleared causing service IP loss.
The kube-proxy crash indicates system configuration issue. Resolution follows standard System remediation pattern.
prevention: Increase kube-proxy memory limits. Monitor iptables rule count.

problem: Kubernetes System fault in namespace 'qa'. Symptoms: Container runtime crash. containerd/dockerd not running. Failed to create pod sandbox. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "node": "worker-2", "resource_type": "Node"}.
final_solution:
ssh worker-2 'sudo systemctl status containerd'
ssh worker-2 'sudo systemctl restart containerd'
kubectl get pods --field-selector spec.nodeName=worker-2
reasoning:
Root cause: containerd crashed due to disk full. Socket file corrupted.
The container runtime crash indicates system configuration issue. Resolution follows standard System remediation pattern.
prevention: Monitor container runtime health. Separate runtime data disk.

problem: Kubernetes Resource fault in namespace 'kube-system'. Symptoms: Pod OOMKilled. Memory limit exceeded. Container restarts increasing. Context: {"namespace": "production", "pod": "api-server-xxx", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.
final_solution:
kubectl set resources deployment api-server --limits memory=512Mi
kubectl describe pod api-server-xxx | grep -A5 OOM
kubectl top pod -n pod-ghi012
reasoning:
Root cause: Memory limit too low for workload. Container killed by OOM killer.
The pod oomkilled indicates resource configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Resource remediation pattern.
outcomes: Pod resolved after memory limit increase
prevention: Set memory requests = 70% of limits. Enable VPA for auto-scaling.

problem: Kubernetes Resource fault in namespace 'kube-system'. Symptoms: CPU throttling detected. High cpu usage. Response latency spike. Context: {"namespace": "staging", "pod": "worker-yyy", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.
final_solution:
kubectl top pods -n pod-jkl345
kubectl set resources deployment worker --limits cpu=1000m --requests cpu=500m
kubectl rollout status deployment worker
reasoning:
Root cause: CPU limit set too low relative to workload demand.
The cpu throttling detected indicates resource configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Resource remediation pattern.
prevention: Profile workload CPU usage. Set limits 2x observed peak.

problem: Kubernetes Resource fault in namespace 'staging'. Symptoms: PVC mount failed. Persistent volume claim pending. Storage quota exceeded. Context: {"namespace": "default", "pod": "db-pod-zzz", "resource_type": "StatefulSet"}.
final_solution:
kubectl get pvc -n pod-def456
kubectl describe pvc db-pvc
kubectl patch resourcequota storage-quota --patch '{"spec":{"hard":{"requests.storage":"100Gi"}}}'
reasoning:
Root cause: StorageClass ResourceQuota exhausted. New PVC could not bind.
The pvc mount failed indicates resource configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Resource remediation pattern.
prevention: Monitor storage usage with alerting at 80% quota utilization.

problem: Kubernetes Resource fault in namespace 'production'. Symptoms: HPA not scaling. Metrics server unavailable. CPU metrics missing. Context: {"namespace": "production", "deployment": "frontend", "resource_type": "HPA"}.

kubectl top nodes
kubectl rollout restart deployment metrics-server -n kube-system
reasoning:
Root cause: Metrics server crashed, HPA lost CPU metrics, scaling disabled.
The hpa not scaling indicates resource configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Resource remediation pattern.
prevention: Add liveness/readiness probes to metrics-server. Set replicas=2.

problem: Kubernetes Resource fault in namespace 'qa'. Symptoms: Node memory pressure. Pods evicted. DaemonSet pod pending. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "node": "worker-1", "resource_type": "Node"}.

reasoning:
Root cause: Node running too many memory-intensive pods, triggering eviction.
The node memory pressure indicates resource configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Resource remediation pattern.

problem: Kubernetes Network fault in namespace 'staging'. Symptoms: DNS resolution failure. CoreDNS pod crashing. Service unreachable by name. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "service": "coredns", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.

kubectl logs -n pod-xyz789 deployment/coredns --previous
kubectl rollout restart deployment coredns -n pod-xyz789
reasoning:
Root cause: CoreDNS crashed due to OOM or config error.

problem: Kubernetes Network fault in namespace 'staging'. Symptoms: Ingress returning 502. Backend service unreachable. nginx upstream error. Context: {"namespace": "production", "service": "frontend", "resource_type": "Ingress"}.
final_solution:
kubectl describe ingress frontend-ingress -n pod-abc123
kubectl get endpoints frontend-svc -n pod-abc123
kubectl edit ingress frontend-ingress -n pod-abc123
reasoning:
Root cause: Service targetPort mismatch: Ingress pointed to wrong backend port.

prevention: Validate ingress backend servicePort matches Service spec.

problem: Kubernetes Network fault in namespace 'staging'. Symptoms: Network policy blocking traffic. Pods cannot communicate cross-namespace. Connection refused. Context: {"namespace": "staging", "resource_type": "NetworkPolicy"}.
final_solution:
kubectl get networkpolicy -A
kubectl describe networkpolicy deny-all -n pod-def456
kubectl apply -f allow-cross-ns-policy.yaml
reasoning:
Root cause: Default deny NetworkPolicy blocked inter-namespace service calls.
The network policy blocking traffic indicates network configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Network remediation pattern.
prevention: Document required traffic flows. Apply allow policies before deny-all.

problem: Kubernetes Network fault in namespace 'qa'. Symptoms: LoadBalancer pending. External IP not assigned. Cloud provider error. Context: {"namespace": "production", "service": "api-lb", "resource_type": "Service"}.

Root cause: Cloud provider quota for LoadBalancer IPs exhausted.

Root cause: CNI daemonset crashed due to kernel version incompatibility.

problem: Kubernetes Scheduling fault in namespace 'default'. Symptoms: Pod stuck in Pending state. No nodes available. Insufficient CPU on all nodes. Context: {"namespace": "default", "pod": "batch-job-zzz", "resource_type": "Job"}.

reasoning:
Root cause: Cluster autoscaler disabled. No node had sufficient CPU for pod request.

problem: Kubernetes Scheduling fault in namespace 'kube-system'. Symptoms: Pod pending due to taint. Node taint mismatch. Toleration missing. Context: {"namespace": "default", "pod": "gpu-workload-aaa", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.
final_solution:
kubectl describe node gpu-node-1 | grep -A5 Taints
kubectl patch deployment gpu-workload -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"tolerations":[{"key":"gpu","operator":"Equal","value":"true","effect":"NoSchedule"}]}}}}'
kubectl get pod gpu-workload-aaa -w
reasoning:
Root cause: GPU node tainted with gpu=true:NoSchedule. Deployment missing toleration.
The pod pending due to taint indicates scheduling configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Scheduling remediation pattern.
prevention: Template GPU deployments with standard GPU toleration from the start.

problem: Kubernetes Scheduling fault in namespace 'dev'. Symptoms: Anti-affinity rules blocking pod placement. Deployment replicas all on same node. Node affinity violated. Context: {"namespace": "production", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.
final_solution:
kubectl describe pod -n pod-xyz789 | grep -A20 Affinity
kubectl get nodes --show-labels
kubectl patch deployment web-app -n pod-xyz789 -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"affinity":null}}}}'
reasoning:
Root cause: RequiredDuringScheduling affinity rule too strict for available nodes.
The anti-affinity rules blocking pod placement indicates scheduling configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Scheduling remediation pattern.
symptoms: Deployment replicas all on same node
prevention: Use PreferredDuringScheduling unless strict isolation required.

problem: Kubernetes Scheduling fault in namespace 'staging'. Symptoms: PriorityClass eviction. Low-priority pod evicted. High-priority pod pending. Context: {"namespace": "default", "resource_type": "PriorityClass"}.

kubectl describe pod evicted-pod | grep Priority
kubectl edit deployment low-priority-app
reasoning:
Root cause: High-priority pod preempted low-priority pod. PriorityClass misconfigured.
The priorityclass eviction indicates scheduling configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Scheduling remediation pattern.
prevention: Assign correct PriorityClass to all workloads. Reserve capacity for critical pods.

problem: Kubernetes Image fault in namespace 'staging'. Symptoms: ImagePullBackOff error. Registry authentication failed. ErrImagePull. Context: {"namespace": "production", "pod": "app-bbb", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.

kubectl create secret docker-registry regcred --docker-server=registry.example.com --docker-username=user --docker-password=pass -n pod-xyz789
kubectl patch deployment app -n pod-xyz789 -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"imagePullSecrets":[{"name":"regcred"}]}}}}'

Root cause: imagePullSecret missing. Registry requires authentication.
The imagepullbackoff error indicates image configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Image remediation pattern.
prevention: Use external secrets operator. Rotate registry credentials quarterly.

problem: Kubernetes Image fault in namespace 'qa'. Symptoms: Image tag not found. 404 from registry. Wrong image version. Context: {"namespace": "staging", "pod": "backend-pod", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.

kubectl describe pod backend-pod -n pod-abc123 | grep Image

reasoning:
Root cause: Deployment using :latest tag which was overwritten. Pin to explicit SHA or semver.

prevention: Never use :latest in production. Pin image digest in deployment spec.

problem: Kubernetes Image fault in namespace 'dev'. Symptoms: Multi-arch image failure. amd64/arm64 mismatch. Container exec format error. Context: {"namespace": "production", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.

Root cause: Image built only for amd64, but cluster has arm64 nodes.
The multi-arch image failure indicates image configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Image remediation pattern.
outcomes: Container starts on all node architectures
prevention: Use buildx multi-arch builds in CI pipeline.

kubectl patch deployment gpu-workload -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"tolerations":[{"key":"gpu","operator":"Equal","value":"true","effect":"NoSchedule"}]}}}}'

problem: Kubernetes Scheduling fault in namespace 'dev'. Symptoms: Anti-affinity rules blocking pod placement. Deployment replicas all on same node. Node affinity violated. Context: {"namespace": "production", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.

kubectl get nodes --show-labels
kubectl patch deployment web-app -n pod-xyz789 -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"affinity":null}}}}'
reasoning:
Root cause: RequiredDuringScheduling affinity rule too strict for available nodes.
The anti-affinity rules blocking pod placement indicates scheduling configuration issue. Resolution follows standard Scheduling remediation pattern.
symptoms: Deployment replicas all on same node
prevention: Use PreferredDuringScheduling unless strict isolation required.

problem: Kubernetes Scheduling fault in namespace 'kube-system'. Symptoms: PriorityClass eviction. Low-priority pod evicted. High-priority pod pending. Context: {"namespace": "default", "resource_type": "PriorityClass"}.

problem: Kubernetes Image fault in namespace 'default'. Symptoms: ImagePullBackOff error. Registry authentication failed. ErrImagePull. Context: {"namespace": "production", "pod": "app-bbb", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.

problem: Kubernetes Image fault in namespace 'qa'. Symptoms: Image tag not found. 404 from registry. Wrong image version. Context: {"namespace": "staging", "pod": "backend-pod", "resource_type": "Deployment"}.

problem: Kubernetes System fault in namespace 'production'. Symptoms: Node NotReady. Kubelet not responding. Disk pressure on node. Context: {"namespace": "kube-system", "node": "worker-node-1", "resource_type": "Node"}.

prevention: Configure log rotation. Set container log max-size in kubelet config.

Root cause: etcd member lost quorum due to network partition. Leader election stalled.
